description: Query directly names a term defined in one of the captured memos. Top result should be that memo with high confidence.
query: where did I write about graded inequality?

candidates.title: Graded inequality — Ambedkar's framing
candidates.description: My working definition contrasted with Ambedkar's usage
candidates.bodyExcerpt: I'm using 'graded inequality' to name the structural pattern where consent appears available but the gradient of cost-of-saying-no is steep. Ambedkar uses the same phrase for the caste-graded hierarchy where each layer has someone below it to look down on...

candidates.title: Doctorow on attention extraction
candidates.bodyExcerpt: Attention is the foundation of all human relations. Convert it into a fungible asset and you've broken the substrate of consent itself.

candidates.title: Cookie-banner fatigue
candidates.bodyExcerpt: We click 'I agree' a hundred times a week. The fiction is that this constitutes consent.

candidates.title: Berners-Lee — Solid spec
candidates.bodyExcerpt: Personal Online Datastores (Pods) let users retain ownership of their data while still enabling rich applications...

candidates.title: consent-as-fiction
candidates.description: Working theme — every passage coded under this header treats consent as ritual rather than choice
candidates.bodyExcerpt: Cluster of passages where consent operates as ceremony rather than as agency. The bar is so low that 'agreement' bears no signal.

notes: Direct lexical AND semantic match. Index 0 should rank first; index 4 (the theme) is a reasonable supporting result.
